_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-8 Electrical Therapy (Revised 3/3/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
level_display: Procedure (All EMS Levels — scope varies by modality)
condition: Electrical Therapy — Defibrillation, Cardioversion, Transcutaneous Pacing
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-8: Electrical Therapy — MDHHS Approved 3/3/2025
purpose: Govern the use of AED, manual defibrillation, synchronized cardioversion, and transcutaneous pacing (TCP). Scope varies by modality and provider level. Pad placement, precautions, and energy selection guidelines are standardized.

sections.title: Universal Precautions Before Electrical Therapy
points: Ensure patient's chest is dry before pad application
Remove nitroglycerin paste or transdermal medication patches from the chest wall
Avoid placing pads directly over an implanted pacemaker or ICD device (position pads at least 1 inch away)
Clear all personnel from patient contact before shock delivery — announce 'Clear' and visually confirm no contact before firing
Preferred pad placement: anterior/posterior (A/P) — anterior pad left of sternum; posterior pad below left scapula
Alternative: anterior/lateral (A/L) — anterior pad right of sternum; lateral pad at left anterior axillary line

sections.title: AED (Automated External Defibrillator)
points: ALL EMS provider levels
DO NOT use AED on patients with a confirmed Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD) — refer to LVAD-Procedure Protocol
Indication: cardiac arrest only
No minimum age or weight restriction for AED use
Pediatric attenuated pads preferred for children — if adult pads are the only option: anterior/posterior pad placement REQUIRED
Anterior/posterior pad placement preferred for all AED use
Follow device prompts — analyze, shock if advised, immediately resume CPR

sections.title: Manual Defibrillation (Unsynchronized)
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-8: Electrical Therapy — Manual Defibrillation
points: Paramedic and Specialist (per MCA)
Indications: ventricular fibrillation (V-fib), pulseless ventricular tachycardia (pVT), unstable irregular wide complex tachycardia
Energy selection per device manufacturer and cardiac arrest protocol
If rhythm is shockable after the 4th defibrillation: contact Medical Control
Refractory V-fib: consider double sequential defibrillation per MCA selection — refer to 7-8(S) Double Sequential Defibrillation Protocol
If only one defibrillator available for refractory V-fib: change pad vector (A/P to A/L or vice versa)
Electrical therapy may be less effective in hypothermic patients — continue HQCPR and warming

sections.title: Synchronized Cardioversion
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-8: Electrical Therapy — Synchronized Cardioversion
points: Paramedic only
Indication: hemodynamically UNSTABLE patient with heart rate ≥ 150/min as a primary cause of hemodynamic compromise
CONTRAINDICATED if heart rate is < 150/min UNLESS ordered by Medical Control
Synchronize the defibrillator to deliver shock on the R-wave (avoid T-wave shock)
Polymorphic VT and Torsades de Pointes: treat with UNSYNCHRONIZED defibrillation (not cardioversion) — refer to Tachycardia Protocol
Sedation may be used per Patient Procedural Sedation-Procedure Protocol if patient is conscious
Energy selection per device manufacturer and tachycardia protocol

sections.title: Transcutaneous Pacing (TCP)
points: Paramedic only
Indication: symptomatic bradycardia per Bradycardia Treatment Protocol
Starting rate: 60 beats per minute
Increase mA (milliamps) to achieve capture:
— Unconscious patient: increase in 20 mA increments until capture
— Conscious patient: increase in 5 mA increments to minimize discomfort while achieving capture
Electrical capture: pacing spike followed by wide QRS and change in T-wave morphology
Mechanical capture: confirm by palpating pulse (radial or femoral) — do not rely solely on ECG capture
Sedation for conscious patients may be used per Patient Procedural Sedation-Procedure Protocol (MCA selection in Bradycardia protocol)

sections.title: Double Sequential Defibrillation (DSD)
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-8: Electrical Therapy — Double Sequential (MCA Optional)
points: MCA Optional — Paramedic only
Refer to Section 7-8(S) Double Sequential Defibrillation Protocol for complete guidance
Requires ≥ 3 prior shocks and availability of a second defibrillator
100% quality assurance/quality improvement review required

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may optionally authorize double sequential defibrillation for refractory ventricular fibrillation. If authorized, refer to Section 7-8(S).
options: Authorized per 7-8(S) Double Sequential Defibrillation Protocol
Not authorized — contact Medical Control for refractory V-fib

deterioration_flags: V-fib refractory after 4 shocks — contact Medical Control; consider double sequential (MCA selection) or vector change
Transcutaneous pacing — failure to achieve mechanical capture (palpable pulse) despite electrical capture requires contact Medical Control
Synchronized cardioversion fails to restore rhythm — reassess rhythm; consider unsynchronized shock if polymorphic or rate-related
Hypothermia with V-fib — continue HQCPR and active rewarming; electrical therapy may be less effective
scope_notes: AED: all EMS provider levels; arrest only; no age/weight minimum; do NOT use on LVAD patients
Manual defibrillation: Paramedic (Specialist per MCA); V-fib, pVT, unstable irregular wide complex
Synchronized cardioversion: Paramedic only; unstable HR ≥ 150; contraindicated < 150 without MC order
TCP: Paramedic only; rate 60 bpm starting; mA titrated to capture (20 mA increments unconscious; 5 mA conscious)
Double sequential defibrillation: MCA Optional, Paramedic only — refer to 7-8(S)
